new_classes.definition_en: A beverage or mixture obtained through fermentation of substrates other than fresh grapes (which would yield heading 2204/2205 wine), barley (yielding heading 2203 beer), or ethyl alcohol (yielding heading 2208 spirits), including cider, perry, mead, saké, and mixtures of fermented beverages with each other or with non-alcoholic beverages, not elsewhere specified or included in the Combined Nomenclature.

A fermentation process that acts on substrates other than fresh grapes (e.g. apple juice yielding cider, pear juice yielding perry, honey yielding mead, rice yielding saké), producing alcoholic beverages classifiable under heading 2206.

A mixture of two or more fermented beverages, or a mixture of one or more fermented beverages with one or more non-alcoholic beverages, not elsewhere specified or included in the Combined Nomenclature, classifiable under heading 2206.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the three principal discriminating criteria of CN 2206: (1) production via fermentation of non-grape substrates (modelled via NonGrapeFermentationProcess and the producedBy restriction); (2) exclusion of wine-of-fresh-grapes (2204/2205) by complementing GrapeFermentationProcess; (3) exclusion of distilled spirits (2208) by complementing DistillationProcess; and (4) mixtures of fermented beverages with each other or with non-alcoholic beverages (modelled via FermentedBeverageMixture2206). However, two aspects remain partially covered: (a) the "not elsewhere specified or included" catch-all condition (which requires reasoning over the entire Chapter 22 exclusion space and cannot be exhaustively expressed in OWL without enumerating all excluded headings); (b) the legal note fragment indicating that fermentation-derived products retain heading 2206 character even after secondary processing — this continuity-of-character criterion is difficult to express declaratively in OWL and is therefore left as a coverage gap. Beer (2203) and ethyl-alcohol (2207) exclusions are also not explicitly stated as complement restrictions, slightly reducing completeness.
